title: ranunculus glechomoides
intro: Ranunculus glechomoides, also known as Creeping Buttercup, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. Ranunculus glechomoides is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. It is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications, such as ointments or creams, to aid in wound healing and skin issues.
